summary: Soundboard‑sourced recording with mostly excellent remastering captures an all‑time‑great Europe ’72 show highlighted by a 45‑minute Dark Star and scorching Caution→Who Do You Love.
review: The Ashley‑Bertha SBD master is crystal‑clear after a brief rough start, letting the band’s legendary 1972 performance shine.  Jerry’s soaring guitar, Phil’s thunderous bass bombs, Pigpen’s organ fire and Bill’s dynamic drum solo all drive an unforgettable set—especially the monster Dark Star and the blistering Caution→Who Do You Love sequence.  Minor hiccups (initial mix wobble, a flub on Morning Dew) are far outweighed by the sheer musical brilliance.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 40 reviews, all but one give five stars and praise both the performance (standout Dark Star, Caution‑WDYL, Pigpen) and the soundboard source; only a few note early mix issues, so the combined show/recording score is near‑perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Remastered by Ashley/Bertha – excellent clarity after an initial ~1:30 of level adjustments; occasional early‑set drop‑outs noted but overall crisp, balanced mix with strong instrument separation.

poor_songs: Mr. Charlie (considered corny by some reviewers)
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set rolls with fun jams and strong Pigpen moments; second set erupts into a 45‑minute Dark Star that morphs into a seamless Caution→Who Do You Love/Truckin' climax, delivering the high‑energy arc fans love from Europe ’72.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for his soaring tone, inventive solos and even backup vocal work; described as ‘perfect’, ‘gain‑heavy wah’, and the driving force of Dark Star.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lauded for “rich in the mix”, massive bass bombs, and standout moments on Morning Dew and during the Caution/DS jams.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals noted as powerful (“Bobby’s voice sailing”), with solid rhythm guitar throughout; occasional praise for his lead on U.J.B. transitions.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s early‑show presence is faint but becomes clear after the mix settles; reviewers say he shines on this recording, especially during improvisational passages.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drum solo highlighted as ‘spirited’; his interplay with Phil and Jerry receives high marks for intensity.
